spec_match.rationale: The connector uses anchor-size() inside inset placement expressions and checks both inset and margin acceptance, matching the Chrome 131 feature boundary.

interactive.rationale: The width slider updates the source card while the connector and label rescale from CSS anchor-size() expressions rather than JavaScript layout math.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept copy and feature-page blurb both describe proportional connector placement driven by anchor-size() in inset properties.

edge_cases.rationale: The page now distinguishes the pre-existing sizing-property support from the Chrome 131 inset and margin support with separate live CSS.supports() probes.

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5203950077476864 entry and the CSS Anchor Positioning spec.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, no raw color literals, no inline event handlers, and explicit foreground/background colors for support states.

summary: Resolved the incorrect ChromeStatus reference and tightened the feature detection. The page now exposes separate sizing, inset, and margin probes, so pre-131 anchor-size() support cannot be mistaken for the Chrome 131 inset/margin expansion.
